methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $1M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Axial Composites is a Seed-stage company in Robotics & Manufacturing founded by Giuseppe Rapisarda, having raised $1M+. The broader sector universe comprises 1 public company and 8 private peers including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

Given the early stage and lack of sufficient narrow-stage comparables within Robotics & Manufacturing—no same-stage or adjacent-stage peers met the three-datapoint threshold for reliable peer median analysis—valuation relies on stage-progression methodology. Using the $1M total raised as a proxy base value and applying a typical Seed-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 2.5x yields an implied value of $2.5M. This single-method approach constrains the triangulation range to $2.0M (low) through $2.5M (median) to $2.9M (high).

The analysis supports a valuation in the $2M–$3M range, centered at $2.5M. Key uncertainties include the lack of same-stage public or private comparables to validate the 2.5x multiplier in this specific sector, the absence of financial performance data (revenue, burn rate, runway), and the early maturity of composites robotics as a category. Future fundraising will benefit from expanded peer benchmarking once additional sector transactions and financials emerge.
